Catch-and-re-throw is the correct pattern when you want to
an error but cannot fully handle it at the current layer. Swallowing errors silently (empty catch blocks) hides bugs.
Error Handling Across the Async Stack
In async/await code, errors propagate up through
calls automatically. A rejection in a deeply nested async function surfaces at the first awaiting
block. It is the right place for cleanup code: closing connections, hiding spinners, resetting flags.
